_TEMPLATE_GUIDE: ASIC RACI TOOL — MASTER IMPORT TEMPLATE (covers every importable structure)
HOW TO USE: edit the sections below, then 📂 Load (replaces your workspace) or
⧉ Merge (appends this file's charts/business cases; deliverables dedupe by
name+type). Every top-level section is OPTIONAL — anything missing is seeded
with defaults. This _TEMPLATE_GUIDE block is ignored by the app; delete it
whenever you like.
ID RULES: ids are free-form strings; they only need to be consistent WITHIN
this file. Cross-references that must line up: (1) node.org / task parties /
artifact ownerRef / workScope reference directorate-division-branch-team ids
defined in 'directorates'; (2) bizCase anchor references a chart id + node id;
(3) inputs/outputs/artifactIds reference artifact ids. Dangling refs never
break an import — they are dropped (artifact refs) or degrade gracefully
(org refs, anchors → standalone).
ORG CHART (custom: null): fixed 4 tiers Portfolio→Program→Project→Task via
nested children[] (deeper levels are trimmed). RACI cells live in node.raci,
keyed by the 7 fixed org columns: hq, cos, mission, infra, cyber, sw,
contacts. Cell values are letter strings ('A', 'R', 'RA', 'CI', ...).
framework: 'raci' | 'rasci'. Valid letters overall: R D A S P C I — letters
outside the chart's framework are kept but flagged by the rule engine.
Per node (all optional except name/raci/children): description; status
('todo'|'doing'|'done'); primaryR (when a row holds 2+ R's, names the column
whose R cascades down as the children's A); org {actor, divisionId,
branchId?, teamId?} — the unit badge (divisionId required or the ref is
dropped; org charts only); inputs/outputs (artifact-id arrays = boundary IO);
documents [{id,name,type,size,dataUrl}] with base64 dataUrl (stored in
IndexedDB after load, embedded again on Save).
Per chart view-state (optional): drillPath (node-id chain to open drilled),
chartZoom (0.4–2.5), chartSize {w,h}|null, chartPos {tierIndex:{x,y}} for
manually dragged panes.
LEGACY SHAPE: a file with top-level {title, activities} and no charts[] still
loads — it is wrapped into charts[0] automatically.
FREE-FORM CHART (custom: {...}): org-agnostic. custom.cols defines the party
columns [{key,label,short}] — node.raci is keyed by THESE keys; custom.tiers
names the levels (any depth). No org refs, no roster links (rows never appear
in the Tasks view).
── bizCases[] (Business Case task flows) ──────────────────────────────────
framework: 'raci' | 'daci' | 'rapid'. anchor: {chartId, nodeId} ties the flow
to one org-chart Task row ('this flow implements that task') — owner and
default parties then cascade in and flow-health rolls up; anchor: null = a
standalone tabletop exercise. tasks[] are the step boxes: raci keyed by the 7
org columns; parties maps a column key → roster ref {actor, divisionId?,
branchId?, teamId?} (the executing unit for that column's role); description /
entry / exit are the card's three text blocks; status; x/y canvas position.
edges[] are directed handoffs {from, to} between task ids; label is the
branch condition ('Yes', 'No', 'approved', ...); artifactIds name the
deliverables that change hands (a step's IO is derived from its edges).
Self-loops and duplicate from→to pairs are dropped.
One entry per real-world deliverable, referenced everywhere by id.
type: 'document' | 'data' | 'decision' | 'approval' | 'briefing' | 'other'.
ownerRef: roster ref or null. On Merge, artifacts dedupe by name+type.
Exactly these 6 keys: ocio, mission, infra, cyber, sw, vendor. Each:
{lead: {id,name}|null, divisions: [{id, name, chief, branches: [{id, name,
chief, teams: [{id, name, chief, people: [{id, name, title}]}]}]}]}.
Depth is optional — divisions may have no branches, branches no teams.
actorLabels: display name per directorate key (shown in Roster/pickers).
columnLabels / columnShort: full + abbreviated header per org column key.
columnActor: org column key → directorate key (or null) — this mapping powers
roster-linked defaults (e.g. a flow step's default executing party).
viewMode: 'chart'|'roster'|'bizcase'|'work'|'help'; activeChartId;
activeBizCaseId; showLegend; rosterMode: 'explore'|'full';
collapsedDirectorates {actorKey:true}; workScope: roster ref preselecting the
Tasks view's unit (or null).

activities.name: Portfolio Activity — Modernize Logistics
activities.description: Top-tier portfolio activity. One A per row; the R here cascades down as the next tier's A.

children.name: Program Activity — Inventory Platform
children.description: Two R's on this row — primaryR names which column's R cascades down as the children's A.

children.name: Project Activity — Barcode Scanning
children.description: Project row assigned to a Branch (org ref goes one level deeper than the Program's Division).

children.name: Task — Integrate scanner SDK
children.description: Deepest tier. This node is the anchor target of the 'SDK Integration' flow below. It consumes and produces registered deliverables (boundary IO) and carries an attached document.

children.name: Task — Site survey
children.description: A sibling task with its own explicit A (overrides the cascaded owner — flagged, not blocked).

children.name: Program Activity — Fleet Telematics
children.description: Minimal sibling program (no org ref, no children yet).

activities.name: Portfolio Activity — Cyber Posture
activities.description: Second top-level row consuming a decision deliverable produced elsewhere.

activities.name: Initiative — Policy Refresh
activities.description: Free-form charts key raci by custom.cols keys, nest to any depth, and carry no org refs.

children.name: Workstream — Draft Directive
children.description: RASCI framework example — the S (Support) letter is valid here.

bizCases.name: EXAMPLE — SDK Integration (anchored flow)
bizCases.description: Anchored to the org chart's 'Task — Integrate scanner SDK' row: the row's cascaded owner shows through on steps with no explicit owner, and its org ancestry seeds default parties.

tasks.name: Request vendor SDK
tasks.description: No explicit A on this step — the anchored chart's owner is inherited (shown dashed).

tasks.name: Security review
tasks.description: Explicit A on a different column than the inherited owner = branch override (warned, never blocked). Party ref demonstrates full team depth.

tasks.name: Integrate & test
tasks.description: Decision point — the two outgoing edges carry Yes/No branch labels.

bizCases.name: EXAMPLE — Standalone tabletop (DACI)
bizCases.description: anchor: null keeps this a standalone exercise — no inherited context, no roll-up. DACI framework: Driver, Approver, Contributor, Informed.

tasks.name: Frame the decision
tasks.description: DACI letters: D drives, A approves.

artifacts.name: Working notes
artifacts.description: Anything that doesn't fit the other types.
